User Guide · {{ meta.version }} · {{ meta.reading_time_min }} min read

{{ meta.title }}

Audience  Articleship · Semi-qualified · Qualified Module  {{ meta.key }} Updated  {{ generated_at }}
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ EXECUTIVE SUMMARY · the one-page version ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

Executive summary

For the busy reviewer

Clause 44 of Form 3CD requires every assessee under tax-audit to report its total expenditure on goods and services for the year, broken up across four GST cohorts — Exempt · Composition · Other Registered · Unregistered. This module ingests the books export (JSON + ledger Excel), applies an ICAI-aligned cascade per voucher line, lets the auditor tag exempt-supply purchase ledgers and pick exclusions, and produces a six-sheet working paper that ties line-for-line to the 3CD schedule.

  • Input  Tally-style accounting JSON (daybook + ledger masters) plus the ledger-mapping XLSX carrying the subhead classification.
  • Effort  8-15 minutes per division once the auditor has done one pass through a book-set.
  • Output  Single Excel with six sheets: Clause 44 Summary, Reconciliation (ICAI 5-line format), Col 3 · Exempt, Col 4 · Composition, Col 5 · Other Registered ITC, Col 7 · Unregistered.
  • Audit assertion supported  Completeness & classification of expenditure under Clause 44, aligned to ICAI Guidance Note on Tax Audit Para 79.1 – 79.21.
What this Readme tells you honestly The books JSON does not carry per-voucher tax rate, nature-of-supply, Section 17(5) eligibility, bill-of-supply markers, or status-at-time-of-supply. This Readme documents exactly how the module works around those absences — and where the auditor's judgement fills the gap.
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ TOC ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

What's inside

  1. Regulatory primer — Clause 44 & ICAI Guidance Note
  2. The four cohorts & the classification cascade
  3. Before you start — prerequisites
  4. Step-by-step walkthrough
  5. Reading the output workbook
  6. Edge cases & common pitfalls
  7. Frequently asked questions
  8. Glossary
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 1 · REGULATORY PRIMER ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

1 · Regulatory primer

Clause 44 of Form 3CD was inserted by the Income-Tax (8th Amendment) Rules, 2018. Operative from FY 2021-22, it requires a detailed break-up of the assessee's total expenditure (P&L debits plus additions to fixed assets) by GST registration status of the supplier — followed by ICAI Guidance Note on Tax Audit (Revised 2025) Paragraphs 79.1 – 79.21.

The statutory table has seven columns; the engine maintains an internal Col 8 for excluded items so the recon ties cleanly:

  1. Col 1 · serial number (auto-assigned; always 1 for the aggregate row).
  2. Col 2 · total amount of expenditure incurred during the year — gross books total per ICAI Para 79.4 (includes capital additions per Para 79.18 and the Col 8 exclusions before they're netted out).
  3. Col 3 · expenditure relating to goods or services exempt from GST.
  4. Col 4 · expenditure on entities under the Composition scheme.
  5. Col 5 · expenditure on other registered entities (taxable supplies under regular GST).
  6. Col 6 · total expenditure on registered entities — arithmetic: Col 3 + Col 4 + Col 5.
  7. Col 7 · expenditure on entities not registered under GST (URD / consumer / RCM / imports).
  8. Col 8 (engine internal) · auditor-elected exclusions — non-cash charges, Sch III items, money / money-securities, capex add-backs (per ICAI Para 79.4 sub-buckets).

Identity: Col 2 = Col 6 + Col 7 + Col 8. The 3CD form itself prints only Cols 1 – 7; Col 8 lives on the Reconciliation sheet so the auditor can defend Col 2.

What the books JSON does not carry Our engine reads the Tally-style accounting JSON and the ledger-mapping XLSX. These are not tax-invoice-level sources, so the following ICAI-desirable signals are absent and must be worked around (see §2):
  • Per-voucher tax rate / nature-of-supply / exempt-supply classification.
  • Bill-of-supply vs tax-invoice markers.
  • Section 17(5) blocked-credit tags.
  • Status-of-supplier at the time of supply (we only have the current party master).
  • Inter-branch / intra-company elimination markers.
{{ section_fb('regulatory') }}
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 2 · THE FOUR COHORTS + CASCADE ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

2 · The four cohorts & the cascade

Clause 44's four reportable cohorts (Col 3 / Col 4 / Col 5 / Col 7) are mutually exclusive and mutually exhaustive across reportable expenditure. Below is the cascade this engine runs per expense line (not per voucher — one voucher can contribute to multiple cohorts if it has multiple expense debits):

  1. Auditor-excluded ledger (Step 3 tick) → Col 8. Wins above everything; lets the recon present per ICAI Para 79.4 (non-cash / Sch III / money / other / capex add-back).
  2. RCM voucher type (voucherTypeName == "Reverse Charge") → Col 7 with an is_rcm flag. ICAI is silent on RCM-specific reporting; CA practice is to treat and disclose separately.
  3. Input A — auditor-tagged exempt purchase ledger → Col 3. The auditor ticks purchase ledgers whose nature is exempt-from-GST (petroleum products, alcoholic liquor for human consumption, life insurance, notified exempt services, etc.) during the wizard's Step 2.
  4. Foreign supplier / import (party country set and ≠ India) → Col 7 with an is_import flag. Defensible: no Indian GSTIN on the supplier side.
  5. Composition dealer (party gstRegistrationType == "composition") → Col 4.
  6. Registered (Regular) vendor with a valid GSTIN:
    • Input B — ITC inference (toggle, default ON): if the voucher carries no ITC-ledger line, the line is presumed to be an exempt supply from a registered vendor → Col 3. This mirrors long-standing CA practice of using ITC availment as a proxy for taxability.
    • If the toggle is OFF or an ITC ledger is present → Col 5.
  7. Everything else (URD, Consumer, blank registration, or no party linked) → Col 7.
How the engine surfaces ITC ledgers (multi-signal) Step 2 / Tab B's candidate pool is built from the union of the books-XLSX mapping and the Tally JSON ledger list, so an ITC ledger that wasn't mapped in the XLSX still appears in the picker. Each candidate is classified input / output / other using three signals, in order of priority:
  1. Name — pattern match on Input ..., Output ..., ITC ..., Cenvat ..., with whitespace collapsed (so SGST IN PUT matches SGST INPUT).
  2. Parent group — Tally users frequently file ledgers under groups like INPUT CREDIT, OUTPUT CREDIT, Defrerred Input Credit regardless of how the leaf is named.
  3. Voucher usage — the engine walks the daybook and tallies how many purchase vs sales vouchers each candidate ledger appears on. A 3:1 dominance with ≥ 3 appearances flips an otherwise-neutral ledger to input (auto-detected even if its name is bespoke like Tax-Cr-Misc-A2).
Subhead override: when name or parent-group strongly signal input/output, the candidate is admitted to the pool regardless of whether the auditor mis-mapped its books-XLSX subhead to Sundry Debtors / Trade Receivables (a common real-world mistake).
Coverage diagnostic on the Schedule tab After Generate, the engine reports the share of registered-vendor purchase vouchers that actually carried an ITC ledger (itc_coverage_pct). When ITC inference is ON and coverage falls below 70%, a yellow advisory banner appears: most likely the auditor missed tagging some input ledgers, so Input B is sweeping value into Col 3 incorrectly. The banner links back to Step 2 with one click.
Why Input A wins over Input B An auditor-tagged exempt ledger (Input A) always routes to Col 3 for that line before Input B is even considered — so a ledger like "Petroleum Purchase" on a vendor that wasn't charging GST can never double-count into Col 3 once via Input A and again via Input B. The report surfaces the Col 3 split — "Input A ≈ ₹X · Input B ≈ ₹Y" — on the Schedule header.
Col 3 · Exempt

Exempt-from-GST supplies

Driven by Input A (auditor-tagged purchase ledgers) + Input B (registered vendor + no ITC entry, if toggle ON). Two-source design: manual precision + heuristic coverage.

Col 4 · Composition

Composition-scheme dealers

Driven purely by the party master's gstRegistrationType. Auditor should spot-check using bill-of-supply markers in paper invoices.

Col 5 · Other Registered

Regular-GST registered vendors

Party master says Regular and has a valid GSTIN. Default "residual taxable" bucket. ITC presence on the voucher is a confirmation signal only — never the gate for Col 5.

Col 7 · Unregistered

URD, Consumer, imports, RCM

No GSTIN, foreign country, RCM voucher type, or blank/consumer registration all fall here. Import & RCM carry separate flags for drill-down.

{{ section_fb('cohorts') }}
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 3 · PRE-REQUISITES ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

3 · Before you start

3.1 The Tally export you'll need

Open the client's books in Tally Prime (or ERP 9) and export the ledger-vouchers JSON for the audit period.

StepTally menu path
1Gateway of Tally → Display More Reports → Account Books → Ledger
2Set the period using Alt + F2 to the full audit year (e.g. 1-Apr-2024 to 31-Mar-2025)
3Press Ctrl + E (Export). Format: JSON. Show profit / Show inventory: No. Range: All ledgers.
4Save the file. The default name is {COMPANY}_DAYBOOK_{from}-{to}.json — keep it as-is; the module reads metadata from the filename.
Pro tip The same JSON powers Clause 44 and 43B(h) and the Balance Confirmation module. Export it once, reuse it three times.

3.2 Client master & period

  • The client must already exist in All Clients. If not, click + Add Client on the dashboard first — that takes 30 seconds.
  • For multi-division clients, ensure each division has been added (Settings → Divisions). Tally usually exports one JSON per division.
  • The audit period should match the year your Tally export covers. Default is the previous FY.

3.3 Access & permissions

Any user with the Auditor role can run the module. Admin rights are not required. The output never leaves your tenant database.

{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 4 · WALKTHROUGH ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

4 · Step-by-step walkthrough

1

Pick the client & start a run

From All Clients, open the client (e.g. ABC Textile Mills) → Utilities → Clause 44.

You land on the Choose Period screen. Pick the FY, choose a division if the client is multi-division, and click Start Run. Upload both files: the books JSON and the ledger-mapping XLSX.

Upload-guard The engine compares the JSON's companyName against the client file using a fuzzy match (token-set / token-sort, ≥ 80 after stripping "P Ltd", "Private Limited", "& Co." etc.). A clear mismatch aborts the upload — you cannot accidentally land ABC Textile Mills books inside the Velav Garments file.
2

Special Ledgers — two tabs

Step 2 in the wizard houses two tabs and one critical toggle.

Tab A · Exempt Purchases (Input A)

Scroll your P&L ledger list; tick the ones whose underlying supplies are exempt from GST by nature — typical examples: Petroleum Purchase, Alcohol Stock, Life Insurance Premium, specified agricultural produce, notified exempt services. Any voucher line hitting these ledgers goes to Col 3, overriding every other rule.

Tab B · ITC Ledgers (Input B)

BS-side picker showing every balance-sheet ledger that could be an input-tax-credit ledger (trade payables / receivables / fixed assets / cash / bank are filtered out). Each row carries up to four chips:

  • INPUT / OUTPUT — the engine's classification of the ledger.
  • via name match · group match · subhead match · voucher usage — provenance, telling you why the engine flagged it. "voucher usage" means the ledger consistently fires on Purchase or Sales vouchers regardless of how it was named.
  • N purchase · N sales — voucher-usage telemetry. Confidence at a glance.
  • unused — the ledger never appeared in any voucher this period.

Pre-ticked = INPUT-kind AND (books-XLSX subhead is Balance with Revenue Authorities / Statutory Dues Payable, OR voucher usage scored input). The "Used in vouchers only" toggle hides dormant ledgers; group headers carry a one-click "Tick all" button so you can bulk-select an entire branch (e.g. all ledgers under INPUT CREDIT).

Output ledgers are never pre-ticked — they fire on sales vouchers, not purchases, so tagging them as "ITC marker" sweeps registered-vendor purchases into Col 3 incorrectly. A red banner appears if any output ledger is ticked.

The "Use ITC inference for Col 3" toggle (default ON)

  • ON — a voucher from a Regular-registered vendor that carries no ITC-ledger entry is presumed an exempt supply and routes to Col 3 (Input B). Reflects long-standing CA practice.
  • OFF — only Input A drives Col 3. All Regular vendors default to Col 5 — the strict ICAI Para 79.13 reading.

Whichever mode you chose is recorded in the working-paper disclaimer.

3

Exclusions

Step 3 is the P&L exclusion picker — tick any ledger that should be excluded from Clause 44 reporting. The engine pre-ticks keyword-matched suggestions (Wages, Salary, Interest, PF/ESI, Provident Fund, etc.) but you are the final authority.

Fixed-asset / capex ledgers are not pre-ticked — ICAI Para 79.18 requires capex to flow into Col 2. The recon sheet (next step) shows capex as an explicit add-back for transparency.

4

Generate & review

Click Generate Report. The engine:

  1. Walks every voucher; for each expense line runs the 6-step cascade from §2.
  2. Aggregates by ledger (expense-head view) and by party (vendor view).
  3. Builds the ICAI 5-line reconciliation (P&L total + capex − non-cash − Sch III − money/securities − other = Col 2).
  4. Auto-categorises excluded ledgers into the 4 ICAI recon buckets (override on the Recon tab via per-line dropdown).

Typical runtime: 1–3 seconds for a 1,000–2,000 voucher book-set.

5

Review the Report

Three tabs:

  • Schedule — a 7-tile clickable KPI strip across the top (Col 2 · Col 3 · Col 4 · Col 5 · Col 6 · Col 7 · Col 8), the seven-column per-ledger / per-party pivot below, and voucher-level drill-downs on every row. Click any KPI tile (or any column header in the pivot) to filter the table to rows that contributed to that bucket — non-zero rows only, sorted by that bucket descending; the active column is highlighted, others dim. A filter banner with a Clear filter link appears between the strip and the pivot. A contextual chip line above the strip shows the Col 3 split (Input A vs B), RCM voucher count, imports total, and the coverage advisory when applicable.
  • Reconciliation — ICAI 5-line format. Every excluded ledger (Col 8) carries an auto-detected sub-bucket (non-cash · Sch III · money · capex add-back · other) with a per-line override dropdown.
  • Disclaimer — free-text editor seeded with the default Para 79.21 boilerplate. Edit per client; the final text is stamped onto the Excel reconciliation sheet on every export.
6

Download the working paper

Top-right of the Report screen, click Export Excel. You get a six-sheet workbook described in the next section. Every sheet carries frozen headers + auto-filters for pivot-ready use.

{{ section_fb('walkthrough') }}
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 5 · OUTPUT ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

5 · Reading the output workbook

SheetContentsTie to 3CD
Clause 44 SummaryAggregate row + per-ledger six-column pivot (Col 2 / Col 3 / Col 4 / Col 5 / Col 6 / Col 7). Frozen header for easy scrolling.Direct copy-paste into the 3CD table.
ReconciliationICAI Para 79.4 five-line recon: P&L total + capex additions − non-cash charges − Schedule III items − money / securities − other exclusions = Reportable Col 2. Ends with the editable disclaimer text.Working-paper proof that Col 2 reconciles to books.
Col 3 · ExemptEvery voucher classified as Col 3, with date, voucher number, division (if multi), ledger, party, GSTIN, country, RCM flag, Amount, Value Eligible for ITC, Reason / Notes, and a blank Auditor Remarks column (per ICAI Para 79.20).Backing for Col 3.
Col 4 · CompositionSame column set, filtered to Composition vouchers.Backing for Col 4.
Col 5 · Other Reg ITCSame column set, usually the largest sheet.Backing for Col 5 / Col 6.
Col 7 · UnregisteredURD vouchers, imports (is_import), and RCM vouchers (is_rcm) — all flagged in the RCM / Country columns.Backing for Col 7.
Value Eligible for ITC — how we compute it Per ICAI Para 79.20 the auditor is expected to call out ITC-eligible value separately. The engine flags a line as ITC-eligible when: (a) a tagged ITC-input ledger appears on the same voucher, AND (b) the voucher is not of type "Reverse Charge", AND (c) the line is not Input-A-sourced (exempt-tagged). Everything else shows zero in that column.
{{ section_fb('output') }}
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 6 · EDGE CASES ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

6 · Edge cases & common pitfalls

6.1 Capital expenditure

ICAI Para 79.18 requires capex to appear in Cols 2 & 5/7 — the engine flows FA-group ledgers into the expenditure pool by default. The recon tab shows "+ Capex additions" as an explicit line for transparency. If you want to exclude capex contrary to ICAI, manually tick the FA ledger on the Exclusions step and override its recon bucket to Capex add-back.

6.2 Reverse Charge (RCM) vouchers

Vouchers with voucherTypeName == "Reverse Charge" are auto-bucketed to Col 7 regardless of vendor status. They carry an is_rcm flag visible in the Excel RCM column and as a chip on the Schedule header. If your firm's policy requires RCM vouchers to follow vendor status instead, uncheck the toggle and classify manually (future releases will expose a firm-wide preference).

6.3 Foreign suppliers / imports

Any ledger whose country field is set and not "India" routes to Col 7 (no Indian GSTIN). The reason column records the country. This is the defensible ICAI position for foreign suppliers; note that IGST paid at customs sits in a separate expenditure head (not auto-classified as import).

6.4 Salaries, PF, ESI, gratuity (Schedule III)

These are not expenditure under Clause 44 per ICAI Para 79.2 — they're excluded via the Exclusions step and auto-categorised to the Schedule III bucket in the recon. The engine's suggestion engine pre-ticks them.

6.5 Depreciation & provisions

Non-cash charges. Excluded on the Exclusions step; auto-categorised to the Non-cash bucket in the recon.

6.6 Interest, TDS, investments

Money / securities transactions are not a "supply" under GST — excluded via the Exclusions step and auto-categorised to the Money bucket in the recon.

6.7 Composition vendors with no marker

The engine relies on gstRegistrationType == "composition" in the party master. If the client accountant didn't maintain this field, Composition vendors leak into Col 5 (Registered). Mitigation: use the expense-wise drill-down, identify such vendors by bill-of-supply inspection, and tag the party ledger manually in Tally before re-exporting.

6.8 Status at time of supply

ICAI Para 79.17 implies the supplier's status at the time of supply is what matters. Our engine only has the current party-master state. If a supplier's GSTIN was active during the supply but has since been cancelled, you would see them as URD today — override via the Exclusions step if material.

{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 7 · FAQ ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

7 · Frequently asked questions

How accurate is the auto-classification?

The engine is deterministic, not probabilistic — there is no ML model. Classification accuracy is a function of (a) how cleanly the client's party master is maintained (GSTIN + gstRegistrationType fields populated), and (b) how carefully the auditor uses Inputs A & B at Step 2. A book-set with a complete party master and the auditor tagging 5-15 exempt-supply purchase ledgers typically needs zero overrides.

Can I reuse overrides from last year?

Per-run. Tagged ITC ledgers and exclusion ticks sit on the run document and can be copied forward via "Start a new run" on the same client in a future period — a future release will add an explicit "Recall last year's mapping" button.

What if Tally has been migrated mid-year?

Export each segment (Apr-Aug from old company, Sep-Mar from new) as separate JSONs and create two runs under the same period with different divisions. The Consolidated view (Report header action) merges them additively.

Does the module support Zoho/Busy/SAP?

Today only Tally-style JSON is supported natively. Other sources need a pre-processing adapter — write to {{ support_email }} with your format sample.

Does it leak data?

No. The books JSON is parsed and stored against your tenant DB. The classification rules are local. No call goes to any external LLM unless you explicitly use the OCR-attach feature in Fixed Assets — that's a different module and uses Gemini Vision under your Emergent LLM key.

What happens when I delete a run?

The run, its selections, and the generated artefacts are all removed. Audit-trail entries (who deleted, when) survive in the admin log.

Can two articled clerks work on the same run simultaneously?

Yes, with caveats. The step saves are idempotent — the last saver wins per field (ITC list, exempt list, exclusions). Coordinate across the bench.

What if the books JSON mis-tags a Composition vendor as Regular?

The engine trusts the party master. Mitigation: use the Report screen's Party-wise drill-down, identify the mis-tagged vendor, correct in Tally, re-export, re-generate. No inline party-master override exists in the utility today.

{{ section_fb('faq') }}
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 8 · GLOSSARY ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

8 · Glossary

3CD
Annexure to Form 3CA / 3CB — the tax-audit report under section 44AB.
Clause 44
The clause inside 3CD that captures the GST-status breakup of total expenditure.
Cohort
Our internal name for one of the four mutually-exclusive Clause 44 classification buckets: Col 3 (Exempt), Col 4 (Composition), Col 5 (Other Registered), Col 7 (Unregistered).
Composition scheme
A simplified GST regime for small suppliers (turnover ≤ ₹1.5 Cr; ≤ ₹50 L for services). Composition vendors charge concessional flat rates and cannot pass on input credit.
Exempt supply
Supply that is exempt from GST — explicitly notified as nil-rated, exempted, or non-taxable. In this module driven by Input A (auditor tag) or Input B (ITC inference).
Input A
Auditor-tagged exempt-supply purchase ledgers. Any voucher line on these ledgers routes to Col 3 first, overriding every other rule in the cascade. Always wins over Input B — no double-count.
Input B (ITC inference)
Rule that routes a Regular-registered vendor's voucher with no ITC-ledger entry to Col 3 as a presumed exempt supply. Controlled by the toggle on Step 2, default ON.
Parent group
The Tally classification ladder above an individual ledger — e.g. Indirect Expenses > Selling & Distribution.
Reconciliation (ICAI Para 79.4)
The 5-line working-paper that ties books to Clause 44: P&L total + capex additions − non-cash charges − Schedule III items − money/securities transactions − other auditor exclusions = Reportable Col 2.
Run
A workspace tied to (client × period × division). Holds the uploaded books JSON + ledger XLSX, classification selections, and the generated working paper.
RCM
Reverse-charge mechanism — GST liability on the recipient instead of the supplier. Voucher-type "Reverse Charge" auto-bucketed to Col 7 with an is_rcm flag.
Disclaimer (Para 79.21)
The ICAI-prescribed qualified note the auditor attaches when classification relies on incomplete data. Editable per-run via Report > Disclaimer tab; stamped into the Excel Reconciliation sheet.
{{ section_fb('glossary') }}
{% endblock %}
